Effective date: April 30, 2001 (28:8 Md. R. 761)

Chapter revised as an emergency provision effective April 3, 2026 (53:9 Md. R. 411); amended permanently effective June 8, 2026 (53:11 Md. R. 484)

para.text: In this chapter, the following terms have the meanings indicated.
Terms Defined.
“Board” means the Maryland Board of Nursing.
“Certificate” means a certificate issued by the Board to practice as a nursing assistant in this State.
“Certified medicine aide (CMA)” means an individual certified in good standing as an active CNA who:
Satisfies the requirements of COMAR 10.39.03.02; and
Is further certified by the Board to accept delegation of administration of certain medications.
Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA).
“Certified nursing assistant (CNA)” means an individual, regardless of title, who, for compensation and subject to regulations adopted by the Board, routinely performs:
Nursing tasks delegated by a registered nurse, licensed practical nurse, or advanced practice registered nurse; or
Technical tasks delegated by a licensed certified midwife.
“Certified nursing assistant (CNA)” does not include dialysis technician or medication technician.
Certified Nursing Assistant-I (CNA-I).
“Certified nursing assistant-I (CNA-I)” means a CNA who:
Meets the requirements set by the Board and federal law; and
May practice in any practice setting.
“Certified nursing assistant-I (CNA-I)” includes a:
CNA who was certified as a geriatric nursing assistant on March 31, 2026;
Certified medicine aide; and
CNA who is certified pursuant to § 8-6A-05(c)(5) of the Health Occupations Article, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Certified nursing assistant-II (CNA-II)” means a CNA who:
On March 31, 2026, was certified as a CNA but not certified as a geriatric nursing assistant; and
May not practice in a nursing facility or skilled nursing facility.
“Controlled schedule drugs” means drugs listed as Schedule I, II, III, IV, or V controlled dangerous substances under Criminal Law Article, §§5-402 — 5-406, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Licensed practical nurse (LPN)” has the meaning stated in Health Occupations Article, §8-101, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Registered nurse (RN)” has the meaning stated in Health Occupations Article, §8-101, Annotated Code of Maryland.
An applicant for certification as a CMA shall provide evidence satisfactory to the Board of meeting the following requirements:
Current certification by the Board as an active CNA-1 in good standing;
Current employment and work experience as required by the Board in a setting acceptable to the Board, including:
Current employment and 2 years of full-time experience as a CNA-1 in a facility licensed under 42 CFR Part 483 et seq.; or
Current employment and 1 year of full-time experience as a CNA-I in a facility licensed in Maryland under COMAR 10.07.02; and
Successful completion as evidenced by a certificate of completion from a Board-approved CMA training program.

The community college shall submit verification of successful completion of the CMA training program to the Board within 5 business days after completion of the program in a manner prescribed by the Board.
Before renewal of certification as a CMA, and subject to the requirements in Health Occupations Article, §8-6A-08, Annotated Code of Maryland, and COMAR 10.39.01.09, the applicant shall:
Successfully complete a Board-approved 8-hour medicine aide continuing education program as required by Regulation .03B of this chapter; and
Provide satisfactory evidence of completion of 100 hours of practice as a certified medicine aide in the 2-year period preceding the date of renewal.

para.text: In this title, the following documents are incorporated by reference.
Documents Incorporated.

“Maryland Medical Protocols for Emergency Medical Services (MIEMSS July 1, 2023 Edition)”. This document can be obtained through the Maryland Institute for Emergency Medical Services Systems at 653 W. Pratt Street, Baltimore, Maryland 21201 (410-706-4449).

“Maryland State Trauma Registry Data Dictionary for Adult Patients (MIEMSS July 1, 2025 Edition)”. This document can be obtained through the Maryland Institute for Emergency Medical Services Systems at 653 W. Pratt Street, Baltimore, Maryland 21201 (410-706-4449).

“Maryland State Trauma Registry Data Dictionary for Pediatric Patients (MIEMSS October 1, 2025 Edition)”. This document can be obtained through the Maryland Institute for Emergency Medical Services Systems at 653 W. Pratt Street, Baltimore, Maryland 21201 (410-706-4449).

"Maryland State Trauma Registry Data Dictionary for Burn Patients (MIEMSS May 21, 2020 Edition)”. This document can be obtained through the Maryland Institute for Emergency Medical Services Systems at 653 W. Pratt Street, Baltimore, Maryland 21201 (410-706-4449).
